title: Samsara API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Samsara API key detected in source code. Samsara is a comprehensive fleet management and IoT platform providing vehicle telematics, GPS tracking, driver safety monitoring, equipment sensors, video dash cams, and compliance solutions for transportation and logistics companies. These API keys (bearer tokens) authenticate programmatic access to Samsara's Cloud API granting the ability to retrieve vehicle location data, access driver safety scores, query equipment sensor readings, download dash cam footage, manage fleet assets, access hours-of-service logs, retrieve maintenance records, configure alerts and webhooks, access historical route data, and export compliance reports. Exposing this API key allows attackers to track real-time vehicle locations revealing operational patterns and trade secrets, access driver personal information including HOS logs and behavior data potentially violating privacy regulations, exfiltrate sensitive business intelligence including customer routes and delivery schedules, download dash cam video footage containing proprietary information or privacy-sensitive recordings, manipulate fleet configurations and alert settings disrupting operations, access fuel consumption data and operational metrics revealing cost structures, consume API quotas causing service disruption to legitimate applications, access maintenance records and equipment data, query historical telematics data for competitive intelligence gathering, and potentially violate FMCSA ELD mandate compliance by accessing or modifying electronic logging device data. IMMEDIATELY revoke this API key from your Samsara dashboard at https://cloud.samsara.com/settings/api-tokens (navigate to Settings → API Tokens → [Token Name] → Delete), generate a new replacement API key with appropriate scopes and permissions (use read-only access for reporting, limit write access to specific resources), audit recent API activity in Samsara dashboard Activity Log for unauthorized requests or suspicious data access patterns, review API call logs for unusual queries or bulk data exports, check for unauthorized webhook configurations or data forwarding integrations, inspect vehicle tracking history for privacy breaches, verify dash cam footage access logs for unauthorized downloads, store API keys securely using environment variables, secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager), or encrypted configuration files (never commit keys to version control or expose in client-side code), implement API key rotation every 90 days as a security best practice, use separate API keys for different applications and environments (development, staging, production) to limit blast radius of compromise, apply principle of least privilege when creating API keys (grant only required scopes: read vs write, specific data types), implement rate limiting and caching in your applications to minimize API calls, monitor Samsara API usage patterns and set up alerts for anomalous activity (sudden spike in requests, access from unexpected IPs or geolocations), ensure compliance with data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, driver privacy laws) when storing or processing telematics data, use Samsara's webhook features with signature verification instead of polling APIs excessively, and implement proper access controls for dash cam footage and driver-sensitive information to protect privacy and prevent unauthorized surveillance.

Samsara API bearer token detected in Authorization header. Samsara provides enterprise fleet management, vehicle telematics, compliance monitoring, and IoT sensor solutions for commercial transportation operations. This bearer token authenticates API requests to Samsara Cloud API enabling access to real-time vehicle locations, driver behavior analytics, dash cam video footage, electronic logging device (ELD) data, equipment sensor readings, maintenance schedules, fuel consumption metrics, compliance reports, and operational dashboards. Exposed bearer tokens grant attackers complete access to your fleet's sensitive operational data including real-time GPS tracking of all vehicles revealing delivery routes and customer locations, driver personally identifiable information (PII) including hours-of-service logs and performance evaluations potentially violating FMCSA privacy requirements, high-resolution dash cam footage containing proprietary business operations and privacy-sensitive recordings, historical telematics data revealing operational patterns and efficiency metrics, fuel consumption and cost data exposing financial information, maintenance records and equipment diagnostics, compliance documentation for FMCSA ELD mandate and DOT regulations, customer delivery schedules and route optimization algorithms, asset inventory and equipment utilization rates, and webhook configurations that could be manipulated to exfiltrate data to external systems. Unauthorized API access enables competitors to analyze your operational efficiency, reverse-engineer routing algorithms, identify high-value customers, and exploit business intelligence. IMMEDIATELY revoke this bearer token from your Samsara account settings at https://cloud.samsara.com/settings/api-tokens (Settings → API Tokens → locate the compromised token → click Delete), generate a new API token with minimal required scopes (prefer read-only access for analytics, limit write permissions to specific resource types), thoroughly audit API access logs in Samsara dashboard Activity Log (Settings → Activity Log) to identify unauthorized requests including unusual query patterns, bulk data exports, or access from unexpected IP addresses or geolocations, review vehicle tracking history for privacy breaches or unauthorized surveillance, inspect dash cam footage access logs to identify any unauthorized video downloads, verify webhook configurations haven't been modified to forward data to external endpoints, check for unauthorized changes to fleet configurations, alerts, or user permissions, rotate all related credentials including any Samsara mobile app tokens or integration credentials, implement secure bearer token storage using environment variables or dedicated secret management platforms (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager) - never hardcode tokens in source code or commit to version control systems, use separate API tokens for different applications with scoped permissions following principle of least privilege (e.g., separate tokens for dashboard reporting vs. dispatch operations), implement API key rotation policy requiring token regeneration every 90 days, enable IP address whitelisting in Samsara API token settings if your infrastructure supports static IPs, implement comprehensive API request logging and monitoring to detect anomalous access patterns, set up alerting for suspicious API activity such as unusual request volumes, access outside business hours, or queries from unexpected geographic locations, ensure compliance with transportation data privacy regulations including FMCSA Part 395 (ELD data privacy), GDPR (EU driver data), CCPA (California driver data), and state-specific commercial driver privacy laws, implement role-based access control (RBAC) within your organization limiting who can generate or access API tokens, use Samsara's webhook functionality with HMAC signature verification instead of continuous API polling to reduce token exposure and improve efficiency, enable two-factor authentication (2FA) on all Samsara administrator accounts, conduct regular security audits of API token usage and permissions, and maintain detailed documentation of which tokens are used by which applications for incident response and access revocation purposes.
